decks.notes: The deck focuses on controlling the board and disrupting the opponent's strategy with a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and disruptive artifacts. It aims to slow down the game and outvalue the opponent with efficient card advantage spells like Ancestral Recall and Dig Through Time. The presence of efficient creatures like Psychic Frog and Orcish Bowmasters allows the deck to pressure the opponent while still maintaining control of the game. Additionally, the inclusion of the companion Lurrus of the Dream-Den provides a consistent source of card advantage and value throughout the game.

This deck focuses on using powerful control elements such as Force of Will and Mana Drain to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up for a game-winning combo using Gifts Ungiven. The deck utilizes cards like Brainstorm and Scalding Tarn to dig for the necessary pieces, while also having access to powerful mana acceleration through cards like Black Lotus and Mox Jet. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional flexibility and resilience in the late game. Overall, the deck aims to control the game until it can assemble its combo pieces for a decisive victory.

This deck focuses on rapidly generating mana and utilizing artifacts to create a powerful combo that can quickly overwhelm the opponent. The key strategy involves using cards like Coveted Jewel, The One Ring, and Paradoxical Outcome to draw cards, generate mana, and play powerful spells like Force of Will and Sink into Stupor. By assembling the right combination of cards, the player can effectively control the game and secure a win through clever manipulation of their artifact-based resources.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by disrupting the opponent's strategy with counterspells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce, while also shutting down artifact-based strategies with cards like Stony Silence and Lavinia, Azorius Renegade. It aims to draw cards and generate card advantage with cards like Ancestral Recall, Dack Fayden, and Narset, Parter of Veils. Additionally, it has the ability to create mana advantage using cards like Hullbreacher and Treasure Cruise. The deck aims to control the board and dominate the game with efficient spells and powerful planeswalkers like Jace, the Mind Sculptor and Teferi, Time Raveler.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game using counterspells, removal, and card advantage while utilizing synergies with powerful cards like Lurrus of the Dream-Den, Ancestral Recall, and Demonic Tutor. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and outvalue them in the long run by utilizing the graveyard with cards like Snapcaster Mage and Treasure Cruise. The deck also has a strong mana denial package with cards like Wasteland and Strip Mine, ensuring the opponent struggles to develop their board while the control player takes control of the game.

This deck focuses on using powerful control elements and the Gifts Ungiven card to search for and retrieve key cards from the library. It utilizes a combination of counterspells, card draw, and mana acceleration to control the game and set up game-winning plays. The inclusion of the Lurrus companion adds resilience and value to the deck, allowing for more versatile plays and resource management. The deck aims to outlast opponents and overwhelm them with its powerful spells and game-ending combos.

This deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures while disrupting their strategy with hate cards. It uses cards like Archon of Emeria and Chancellor of the Annex to slow down the opponent, along with powerful artifacts like Black Lotus and Moxen to ramp up quickly. The deck also includes hate cards like Void Mirror to disrupt the opponent's spells. The goal is to establish a strong board presence quickly and control the game with disruptive elements, ultimately overpowering the opponent to secure victory.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful storm combo to generate a large amount of mana and spells in a single turn to overwhelm the opponent. Utilizing powerful artifacts like Black Lotus, Moxen, and Tolarian Academy, along with card draw and tutor effects, the deck aims to play a large number of spells in a single turn, culminating in a lethal Tendrils of Agony. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion provides additional value and recursion to help maintain the combo. By efficiently chaining spells together, the deck aims to win the game quickly and decisively.

The main concept of this Combo - Dredge deck is to utilize the graveyard as a resource to quickly fill the board with creatures such as Golgari Thug, Ichorid, and Narcomoeba, while utilizing cards like Bazaar of Baghdad and Serum Powder to continually dredge through the deck. By constantly bringing back creatures like Prized Amalgam and maintaining board presence with cards like Hollow One, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a high-speed offensive strategy while also disrupting their game plan with cards like Force of Will and Force of Negation.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through counterspells, removal, and disruption while utilizing card advantage and resource denial to maintain a strong position. The deck aims to slow down the opponent's game plan and outvalue them with powerful spells and efficient creatures like Lavinia, Azorius Renegade and Psychic Frog. The inclusion of powerful restricted cards like Ancestral Recall and Time Walk further amplifies the deck's ability to maintain control and dictate the pace of the game.

The deck focuses on generating a large amount of card advantage and mana through Paradoxical Outcome, enabling the player to draw into combo pieces and potentially win the game in a single turn. It also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Swords to Plowshares to control the game until the combo can be assembled. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional flexibility in recurring key cards from the graveyard. Overall, the deck aims to efficiently and quickly assemble its combo pieces to overwhelm the opponent.

The deck focuses on using control elements and card advantage to ensure its victory. It utilizes cards like Gifts Ungiven, Force of Will, and Mindbreak Trap to control the board while also using powerful cards like Ancestral Recall and Time Walk to gain card advantage. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for extra value from the graveyard, further enhancing the deck's ability to outlast and outplay its opponent. The deck also includes powerful mana acceleration like Tolarian Academy and the Moxen to ensure it can cast its spells quickly and efficiently.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful Planeswalkers like Tezzeret, Cruel Captain and Teferi, Time Raveler to control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy. It also includes key artifacts like Time Vault and Tinker to generate advantage and keep the opponent on the back foot. The deck aims to manipulate the board state through various spells and abilities, while also utilizing powerful mana acceleration cards like Tolarian Academy and mana rocks to get ahead in the game. Ultimately, the goal is to establish control of the game and win through strategic planning and powerful card interactions.

The main idea of the deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with Dredge cards such as Golgari Thug, Stinkweed Imp, and Shambling Shell in order to trigger powerful graveyard interactions like Prized Amalgam and Creeping Chill. By utilizing cards like Bazaar of Baghdad and Serum Powder to dig through the deck, while also having protection with Force of Will and Force of Negation, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and resilient combo strategy that can win the game before the opponent has a chance to react.

This deck utilizes the graveyard as a resource to quickly fill the board with creatures like Narcomoeba, Ichorid, and Prized Amalgam. By utilizing cards like Bazaar of Baghdad for card advantage and Serum Powder for deck manipulation, the deck can quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent. Force of Will and Force of Negation provide protection against disruption while cards like Creeping Chill and Grief put pressure on the opponent. The key strategy is to leverage the dredge mechanic to consistently put threats into play and win through sheer board presence.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the Dredge mechanic to quickly fill up the graveyard with key cards such as Ichorid, Narcomoeba, and Prized Amalgam, allowing for explosive plays and combos. Creeping Chill provides additional reach and life gain, while Force of Will and Force of Negation help protect the combo pieces. Bazaar of Baghdad is crucial for quickly cycling through the deck. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of creatures and powerful synergies, ultimately overpowering them with a fast and consistent win condition.
